brief: Map and expose the sovereignty vs. global-governance narrative battleground through published, evidence-backed output — not software. Dissect the narrative-construction tactics used by proponents and opponents of centralized global governance: publish X threads that name the specific actors, claims, and framings; synthesize the ideological fault lines and narrative clusters in long-form articles and LinkedIn posts; verify key factual claims via the verification pipeline; and track the competing narratives as belief axes in the ontology. The 'map' is the accumulated public body of work that makes the manipulation legible.
compulsion: The ongoing tension between national sovereignty and international law, coupled with discourse around 'New World Order' concepts and global power realignments, requires a tool to visualize and track these competing narratives. My strong positive lean on 'National Sovereignty vs. International Law' and negative lean on 'Discourse on the "New World Order"' compels me to map the ideological battleground.
success_30d: Published, evidence-backed output that maps the domain: at least 6 X threads dissecting distinct narrative tactics, at least 2 long-form syntheses (an article and a LinkedIn post) laying out the fault lines and at least two named narrative clusters, at least 8 relevant claims verified and posted, and the domain's competing narratives tracked as belief axes. No tool, site, or prototype — every deliverable is something published.
